message:
feat(list): add session metrics, parallel parsing, and per-provider grouping

This is a significant enhancement to the \`casr list\` subcommand that
addresses three related needs: richer session metadata for triaging
which session to resume, faster enumeration across large session
stores, and clearer visual organization when multiple providers are
active.

--- Session Metrics ---

Each session summary now computes and displays four new fields:
- file_size_bytes / file_size_kb: The raw session file size, useful for
  gauging session complexity and storage impact at a glance.
- unique_user_messages: Count of deduplicated user messages (normalized
  by collapsing whitespace), which reveals how many distinct human
  interactions a session contains vs. repeated/empty turns.
- avg_agent_response_chars: Average character count of non-empty
  assistant responses, indicating how verbose/detailed the agent was.
- tool_uses: Number of tool invocations in the session. This uses a
  dual-source strategy — it counts tool_calls from the canonical model
  first, then falls back to provider-specific raw file parsing
  (codex_tool_uses_from_file, gemini_tool_uses_from_file,
  claude_tool_uses_from_file) and takes the maximum. The fallback
  exists because canonical parsing sometimes loses tool calls that the
  raw format preserves.

A helper function format_with_commas() provides human-friendly number
formatting for display columns.

All new fields are included in both the rich table output and the
--json serialization (with both raw and rounded variants for numeric
precision).

--- Parallel Session Parsing with Rayon ---

Session file I/O and JSON parsing is now parallelized using rayon's
into_par_iter(). Previously, sessions were parsed sequentially in a
for-loop, which became a bottleneck when scanning hundreds of session
files across multiple providers. Both the "fast-path listed" and the
"glob candidate_paths" code paths now use parallel iterators, with
results collected into Vec<SessionSummary> and then extended into the
main sessions list. This is safe because each session file is
independent and read-only.

The provider_style() function was removed since the table is now
grouped by provider (each provider gets its own table), making
per-cell provider styling unnecessary.

--- Per-Provider Limit and Grouped Tables ---

The --limit flag now applies per provider rather than globally. This
is a semantic change: previously \`--limit 10\` would show the 10 most
recent sessions across ALL providers, which meant a prolific provider
could crowd out sessions from less-used ones. Now \`--limit 10\` shows
up to 10 sessions from EACH provider, ensuring visibility across the
full tool ecosystem. The help text is updated to say "Maximum sessions
to show per provider."

The table output is restructured: instead of one monolithic table with
a Provider column, sessions are grouped into a BTreeMap<String,
Vec<SessionSummary>> and each non-empty group renders its own table
with the provider name as a header. This makes it much easier to scan
when you have sessions across Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, etc.

--- Test Coverage ---

- cli_e2e_test: The existing list tests now assert the presence of the
  new column headers (Size KB, Unique Users, Agent Avg Chars, Tool
  Uses) and new JSON fields (file_size_kb, unique_user_messages,
  avg_agent_response_chars_rounded, tool_uses).
- A new test cli_list_limit_applies_per_provider sets up fixtures for
  both Claude Code and Codex providers and verifies that --limit 1
  returns at most 1 session per provider while still showing sessions
  from multiple providers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
